channel.title: Engineering｜企業テックブログRSS
channel.description: Engineeringセクションのブログ更新をまとめたRSSフィード

item.title: Online index migration and shard-scaling in OpenSearch with AOSC | Inside Atlassian
item.description: Learn how Atlassian built AOSC, an OpenSearch plugin to reshape indices and scale their shards online. AOSC is a zero-downtime* index migration &amp; scaling engine for OpenSearch, built as an OpenSearch
item.content:encoded: Learn how Atlassian built AOSC, an OpenSearch plugin to reshape indices and scale their shards online. AOSC is a zero-downtime* index migration &amp; scaling engine for OpenSearch, built as an OpenSearch plugin. It’s open source (Atlassian Labs repo, docs), and this post is the story of how, and why, we built it. Anyone who has […]The post Online index migration and shard-scaling in OpenSearch with AOSC appeared first on Inside Atlassian.
item.author: Christopher Cheung

_custom.originalTitle: Online index migration and shard-scaling in OpenSearch with AOSC

item.title: How AI Rebuilt Salesforce’s Decades-Old Localization Pipeline | Salesforce Engineering Blog
item.description: In our Engineering Energizers Q&amp;A series, we highlight the engineering minds driving innovation across Salesforce. Today, we spotlight Teresa Marshall, Vice President of Localization. Teresa’s team de
item.content:encoded: In our Engineering Energizers Q&amp;A series, we highlight the engineering minds driving innovation across Salesforce. Today, we spotlight Teresa Marshall, Vice President of Localization. Teresa’s team delivers every Salesforce release in up to 34 languages. As Salesforce engineering accelerated, product volume grew by more than 35% between releases while localization budgets and release windows remained […]The post How AI Rebuilt Salesforce’s Decades-Old Localization Pipeline appeared first on Sale
item.author: Scott Nyberg

_custom.originalTitle: How AI Rebuilt Salesforce’s Decades-Old Localization Pipeline

item.title: Introducing Cache Response Rules | The Cloudflare Blog
item.description: Perhaps you’ve seen something that should sail out of cache get dragged back to the origin by a stray Set-Cookie or Cache-Control, headers that can be difficult to change on the origin itself. Cache R
item.content:encoded: Perhaps you’ve seen something that should sail out of cache get dragged back to the origin by a stray Set-Cookie or Cache-Control, headers that can be difficult to change on the origin itself. Cache Response Rules is the fix, applied at the right time.
item.author: Alex Krivit

item.title: Inside AI Builders Week: How teams work in the AI era | Inside Atlassian
item.description: Each quarter, we ask some of our R&amp;D teams to take a week away from the day-to-day to experiment and innovate on AI. We call this AI Builders Week, and it helps fuel our AI transformation. In past qua
item.content:encoded: Each quarter, we ask some of our R&amp;D teams to take a week away from the day-to-day to experiment and innovate on AI. We call this AI Builders Week, and it helps fuel our AI transformation. In past quarters, we’ve focused on building for individual workflows — how do we each get faster and more productive […]The post Inside AI Builders Week: How teams work in the AI era appeared first on Inside Atlassian.
item.author: erooney@atlassian.com

_custom.originalTitle: Inside AI Builders Week: How teams work in the AI era

item.title: Bitbucket Tests now supports tracking tests from multiple branches | Inside Atlassian
item.description: Tracking tests on non-default branches was one of the most requested capabilities during the Bitbucket Tests (beta). We heard you, and today, we’re shipping it. Most teams don’t ship from main. Integr
item.content:encoded: Tracking tests on non-default branches was one of the most requested capabilities during the Bitbucket Tests (beta). We heard you, and today, we’re shipping it. Most teams don’t ship from main. Integration happens on develop, stabilisation on staging, releases cut from release. Until now, Bitbucket Tests only tracked the tests from default(main) branch, leaving the […]The post Bitbucket Tests now supports tracking tests from multiple branches appeared first on Inside Atlassian.
item.author: dhom

_custom.originalTitle: Bitbucket Tests now supports tracking tests from multiple branches

item.title: Stop stitching together HR tools. Start Connecting Them. | Inside Atlassian
item.description: HR doesn’t have a tool problem, it has a connection problem. The Atlassian Ecosystem turns fragmented systems into one intelligent system of work. HR has quietly become one of the most cross-functiona
item.content:encoded: HR doesn’t have a tool problem, it has a connection problem. The Atlassian Ecosystem turns fragmented systems into one intelligent system of work. HR has quietly become one of the most cross-functional operations in any organization. Onboarding a new hire touches IT provisioning, payroll, compliance training, manager coordination, and knowledge management, often across a half-dozen […]The post Stop stitching together HR tools. Start Connecting Them. appeared first on Inside Atlassian.
item.author: Christopher Cheung

_custom.originalTitle: Stop stitching together HR tools. Start Connecting Them.

item.title: Give AI agents the full picture with Loom video prompts | Inside Atlassian
item.description: Record your screen, talk through what you want done, and let Loom generate a structured action plan agents can use to execute tasks.The post Give AI agents the full picture with Loom video prompts app
item.content:encoded: Record your screen, talk through what you want done, and let Loom generate a structured action plan agents can use to execute tasks.The post Give AI agents the full picture with Loom video prompts appeared first on Inside Atlassian.
item.author: sonar

_custom.originalTitle: Give AI agents the full picture with Loom video prompts

item.title: How AI Reduced Customer Bug Triage from Nearly a Year to Less Than a Week | Salesforce Engineering Blog
item.description: By Priya Sethuraman, Abhishek Ghose, Lovish Agarwal, and Aditya Pandey. In our Engineering Energizers Q&amp;A series, we highlight the engineering minds driving innovation across Salesforce. Today, we spo
item.content:encoded: By Priya Sethuraman, Abhishek Ghose, Lovish Agarwal, and Aditya Pandey. In our Engineering Energizers Q&amp;A series, we highlight the engineering minds driving innovation across Salesforce. Today, we spotlight Priya Sethuraman, Director of Software Engineering. Priya leads the Sales Cloud BugWiser initiative, an AI-powered system that standardizes customer bug classification and transforms customer bug signals into […]The post How AI Reduced Customer Bug Triage from Nearly a Year to Less Than a Wee
item.author: Scott Nyberg

_custom.originalTitle: How AI Reduced Customer Bug Triage from Nearly a Year to Less Than a Week

item.title: How the 2026 World Cup affected Internet traffic | The Cloudflare Blog
item.description: We analyzed global HTTP traffic to explore how kickoff times, streaming habits, and hydration breaks reshaped online activity worldwide. From late-night traffic surges to halftime browsing spikes, her
item.content:encoded: We analyzed global HTTP traffic to explore how kickoff times, streaming habits, and hydration breaks reshaped online activity worldwide. From late-night traffic surges to halftime browsing spikes, here is how the world connected during the global tournament.
item.author: Sabina Zejnilovic

_custom.originalTitle: How the 2026 World Cup affected Internet traffic

item.title: Analyzing the evidence that helps businesses win “product not received” disputes | Stripe Blog
item.description: To understand what can influence win rates, we analyzed evidence packets from one million disputes over a 16-week period. Here’s what the data shows and what it means for how you mitigate disputes.
item.content:encoded: To understand what can influence win rates, we analyzed evidence packets from one million disputes over a 16-week period. Here’s what the data shows and what it means for how you mitigate disputes.

_custom.originalTitle: Analyzing the evidence that helps businesses win “product not received” disputes

item.title: Cloudflare Internal DNS is now generally available | The Cloudflare Blog
item.description: Cloudflare Internal DNS brings authoritative and recursive DNS for private networks to the same global network and control plane that runs Cloudflare's Zero Trust, networking, and public DNS.
item.content:encoded: Cloudflare Internal DNS brings authoritative and recursive DNS for private networks to the same global network and control plane that runs Cloudflare's Zero Trust, networking, and public DNS.
item.author: Enrique Somoza

_custom.originalTitle: Cloudflare Internal DNS is now generally available

item.title: Materialized metrics in Prometheus | RedditEng
item.description: Written by Aleksandr Krivoshchekov and Walther Lee. Prometheus has a simple idea at its core: metric_name + labels = time_series. Any two samples with the same metric name and labels map to the same s
item.content:encoded: Written by Aleksandr Krivoshchekov and Walther Lee. Prometheus has a simple idea at its core: metric_name + labels = time_series. Any two samples with the same metric name and labels map to the same series, but adding even a single extra label initiates a completely new series. In Kubernetes, that extra label is usually pod. It’s noisy and always changing. It spams our system with more and more series, slowing down queries and draining memory. It’s mostly pointless for our users. But it’s also c

enclosure.alt: From the RedditEng community on Reddit: "Materialized metrics in Prometheus"

item.title: Content Ingestion &amp; Podcast Video Incident Report | Spotify Engineering
item.description: Over the past two months, podcast creators have experienced a series of reliability issues on Spotify. This...The post Content Ingestion &amp; Podcast Video Incident Report appeared first on Spotify Engin

item.title: How our universal content processing platform Riviera evolved for AI and beyond | Dropbox Tech Blog
item.description: Riviera is the Dropbox content processing platform that’s been iteratively improving content transformation in our products for roughly a decade.
item.content:encoded: Riviera is the Dropbox content processing platform that’s been iteratively improving content transformation in our products for roughly a decade.

item.title: Cloudflare WAF protects WordPress applications from two high-severity vulnerabilities | The Cloudflare Blog
item.description: Cloudflare has deployed two WAF rules in response to high-severity vulnerabilities disclosed to us by the WordPress security team. The new rules protect all Cloudflare customers using affected WordPre
item.content:encoded: Cloudflare has deployed two WAF rules in response to high-severity vulnerabilities disclosed to us by the WordPress security team. The new rules protect all Cloudflare customers using affected WordPress versions, but customers should still update immediately to a patched release
item.author: Daniele Molteni

_custom.originalTitle: Cloudflare WAF protects WordPress applications from two high-severity vulnerabilities

item.title: Closing the Loop: How to Build Self-Improving AI Systems with Automated Feedback Loops | Salesforce Engineering Blog
item.description: After six cycles, the system ran out of things to fix. That sentence probably raises more questions than it answers. Here is how we got there. The first skill PR took three days to merge. Twelve comme
item.content:encoded: After six cycles, the system ran out of things to fix. That sentence probably raises more questions than it answers. Here is how we got there. The first skill PR took three days to merge. Twelve comments on structure. Six on conventions. The contributor fixed everything, merged, moved on. The next contributor hit the same […]The post Closing the Loop: How to Build Self-Improving AI Systems with Automated Feedback Loops appeared first on Salesforce Engineering Blog.
item.author: Scott Nyberg

_custom.originalTitle: Closing the Loop: How to Build Self-Improving AI Systems with Automated Feedback Loops

item.title: The cost of saying yes has changed | The latest from GitHub's engineering team - The GitHub Blog
item.description: The cost of writing code dropped; the cost of owning it didn't. A framework for deciding which changes are actually cheap in the AI era.The post The cost of saying yes has changed appeared first on Th
item.content:encoded: The cost of writing code dropped; the cost of owning it didn't. A framework for deciding which changes are actually cheap in the AI era.The post The cost of saying yes has changed appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.author: Dalia Abuadas

_custom.originalTitle: The cost of saying yes has changed
_custom.blogTitle: The latest from GitHub's engineering team - The GitHub Blog
